taxRateNote: Inside-city rate held steady for 2026 (covers ~88% of households). Annexed-area rate is 5 cents higher. Municipal portion only.
sourceLabel: City of Fredericton 2026 General Fund Operating Budget (program-area summary, adopted 2025-11-24)
dataStatusNote: Transcribed from the eight program-area lines published on the City of Fredericton Budget & Finance page for the 2026 General Fund Operating Budget. The city's own headline total of $184.8M differs from the sum of program areas ($193.0M) because the headline nets out internal allocations (chiefly capital-from-operating recoveries inside Sustainable Infrastructure); the gross program-area figures are used here for like-for-like comparison with Moncton's gross departmental rollup. Water and Sewer Utility ($25.6M) is funded separately and is excluded.

nativeDepartments.name: Public Safety (Fredericton Police Force, Fire Department, by-law enforcement, emergency preparedness, animal control)
Mobility (roads, sidewalks, trails, transit, parking, fleet)
Funded separately via the Fredericton Water and Sewer Utility ($25.6M, paid on the utility bill, not property tax)
Livable Community (recreation, parks, community programs, beautification, culture)
Economic Prosperity & Growth Planning (planning, housing, heritage, economic development)
Corporate Services (finance, HR, IT, legal, clerk, communications)
Governance & Civic Engagement (Council, leadership, First Nation relations)
Sustainable Infrastructure (capital-from-operating, asset management, debt servicing)
Environmental Stewardship (solid-waste management, stormwater, arboriculture / urban forestry)
